rationale.completeness: The PR adds tool call success rate tracking as required, but the --parallel flag already existed and only gets a minor help text update and range validation; the core parallelism configuration work is minimal.
rationale.correctness: The success rate calculation at the per-question level uses tool_calls_count as denominator but successful/failed counts come from tool_calls metadata list, which can diverge if tool_calls_count is tracked separately from the metadata list length, creating a subtle inconsistency. The 100.0 default for zero tool calls is also semantically questionable.
rationale.style: Follows existing patterns well—new fields are added consistently across all result paths (success, timeout, error), aggregation follows the same accumulator pattern, and print formatting matches surrounding code.
rationale.quality: The changes are straightforward and readable, but the tool call success rate logic is duplicated across per-question and per-type aggregation without abstraction, and the by_type accumulator storing lists of successful/failed counts rather than running totals adds unnecessary overhead.
